summary: Well-structured functions and test design maximize Playwright's auto-waiting, parallel execution, and debugging capabilities while keeping tests fast, reliable, and maintainable.
reasoning: Auto-waiting eliminates flakiness caused by explicit sleeps and race conditions
Page Object Model reduces duplication and makes tests resilient to UI changes
Web-first assertions auto-retry until timeout, dramatically reducing flaky test failures
Small focused tests are easier to debug, parallelize, and maintain
API-based test setup is faster and more reliable than navigating through UI for preconditions
Fixtures provide deterministic setup/teardown with proper scoping and dependency injection
